sources.title: Directive Ashes of Peace Campaign v0.2

missionFrame.function: Open the Ashes of Peace campaign with a rescue-and-authentication problem where Starfleet authority may be counterfeit.
missionFrame.dramaticQuestion: How does Starfleet act when its own authority may be counterfeit?

forbiddenOutcomes: reveal Pale Lantern in the opening frame
make a senior officer death unavoidable
treat missing protocol words as player incompetence

phases.title: Initial Reception
phases.summary: Priya receives a fragmented Relief Convoy Twelve distress packet before the formal Asterion reception can begin.
phases.directorPurpose: Put the XO in command of the first response without making the player pass a Starfleet protocol quiz.

phases.title: Convoy Approach
phases.summary: The Breckinridge closes or holds based on the first posture while authentication, scans, quarantine prep, and rescue readiness continue.
phases.directorPurpose: Carry the first response posture into concrete approach tradeoffs.
exitSignals: Boarding, remote verification, or rescue contact threshold is defined

phases.title: First Posture Decision
phases.summary: The command team translates initial findings into the first committed boarding, quarantine, evidence, or security threshold.
phases.directorPurpose: Make the player own the first real operational threshold after the opening brief.
exitSignals: First committed response is ready to resolve

phases.title: First Committed Response
phases.summary: The first response creates persistent rescue, security, evidence, and diplomatic state for the rest of Chapter 1.
phases.directorPurpose: Persist the consequences of the opening posture into the flexible simulation.
exitSignals: Chapter 1 pressure state has enough committed input for the next scene

facts.summary: Priya receives a fragmented distress packet from Relief Convoy Twelve before the formal Asterion reception.
Relief Convoy Twelve is powered and in formation, but no one answers hails.
Several escape craft are missing, and one transport shows a minor plasma leak.
The quarantine beacon carries a technically valid Starfleet emergency authority code that does not match current routing.
No biological signature is confirmed at range.
The apparent Starfleet order contains authentic fragments embedded in a forged signal chain.
There is no pathogen aboard the convoy; the quarantine warning is being used to delay outside discovery.
A Compact recovery team has detained officers and removed a container of transponder modules.

clocks.label: Rescue Window
advanceTriggers: remote verification takes priority over approach
decreaseTriggers: the Breckinridge closes while preserving quarantine readiness

clocks.label: Security Exposure
decreaseTriggers: security posture is explicit and non-escalatory

clocks.label: Evidence Volatility
decreaseTriggers: raw signal and computer evidence are preserved
forensic access is protected before rescue disruption

actorIntentions.summary: Anyone still alive aboard the convoy needs aid before conditions degrade.
directives: Do not make rescue impossible through delay

actorIntentions.summary: The hidden recovery team wants the silence and quarantine confusion to last long enough to escape scrutiny.
directives: Do not reveal this actor intention to the player before discovery

actorIntentions.summary: The Breckinridge command team needs to protect rescue, evidence, and lawful authority at the same time.
directives: Let Command Competence handle routine procedure

readinessGates.motive: The distress packet and plasma leak create plausible risk to life.
readinessGates.information: The player knows the convoy is powered, silent, and partly damaged.
readinessGates.opportunity: The Breckinridge is close enough to choose approach posture now.
readinessGates.resources: Medical, engineering, flight, operations, and science can begin routine response.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Rescue can start while uncertainty remains if risk is logged.
readinessGates.timing: The opening frame is the right moment to set first response posture.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: Immediately after Priya receives the packet.
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: Before the Breckinridge commits to boarding or holding away.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before convoy approach resolves into boarding threshold.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After the first posture is committed.

readinessGates.motive: The quarantine authority code does not align cleanly with current routing.
readinessGates.information: The player can know the signal requires authentication without knowing the hidden cause.
readinessGates.opportunity: Operations and science can verify while the XO sets posture.
readinessGates.resources: Priya and Rowan have relevant lanes.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Delay may cost rescue time, but blind acceptance risks evidence and authority.
readinessGates.timing: This pressure should surface in the opening Command Brief.

actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before direct boarding or system shutdown.

readinessGates.motive: The hidden team benefits from Starfleet delay and confusion.
readinessGates.information: The player does not know the actor exists yet.
readinessGates.opportunity: The team can act only if approach and evidence posture leave room.
readinessGates.resources: The hidden team has local access and removed hardware.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: They avoid direct exposure until pressured.
readinessGates.timing: This pressure should not fire in the opening frame unless the player creates an obvious opening.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: After the Breckinridge approach posture is known.
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: Before evidence custody is locked down.
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: After the team has been exposed or escaped.

selectionPriorities: Prefer the current command posture decision.
Prefer player-safe rescue, authentication, quarantine, security, and evidence pressures.
Do not surface hidden Compact or Lantern truth before discovery.

routineProcedures.summary: Log the distress packet, preserve the raw signal, begin authentication, and task long-range sensors.
Alert Sickbay and Engineering to prepare for possible rescue, quarantine triage, and damage control.

professionalKnowledge.summary: A valid-looking quarantine order still requires reconciliation with current routing and mission authority.
Emergency rescue authority permits faster intervention when delay threatens life, but the accepted risk should be logged.
The XO must choose the initial response posture.
There is no pathogen; the quarantine order is masking the hidden recovery operation.

domainReports.summary: The certificate chain technically validates, but the routing and authority context do not line up with current Starfleet records.
domainReports.recommendation: Keep authentication running while the XO decides how much rescue risk to accept.

domainReports.summary: A powered silent convoy may be a trap or a coerced silence; remote reconnaissance before boarding is tactically prudent.
domainReports.recommendation: Do not board until there is at least a security threshold and a quarantine basis.

domainReports.summary: No biological signature at range is not proof that no biological risk exists.
domainReports.recommendation: Treat the absence of a signature as uncertainty, not clearance.

domainReports.summary: Quarantine preparation should proceed until medical evidence clears it; rescue speed and isolation are not the same decision.
domainReports.recommendation: Prepare isolation and triage together so rescue does not require a blind medical waiver.

domainReports.summary: Automatic shutdown or forced overwrite could erase volatile computer evidence before the cause of the silence is known.
domainReports.recommendation: Preserve volatile system state before any broad shutdown order.

domainReports.summary: Director-only: the Compact recovery team detained officers and removed a container of transponder modules.

commandQuestions.summary: How quickly to close, under what quarantine and security posture, and how to balance rescue against evidence preservation.

warningRules.proposedAction: Move unknown survivors into unrestricted ship spaces before medical isolation.
warningRules.standardConcern: Unknown medical risk should be isolated until assessed.
warningRules.knownConsequence: Bypassing isolation could expose the ship if the quarantine warning is real.
warningRules.availableBasisForException: Immediate life-saving emergency may justify faster transport with recorded risk.

warningRules.proposedAction: Open fire on a civilian or Compact vessel before a hostile act or lawful emergency basis is established.
warningRules.standardConcern: Starfleet weapons use requires a lawful defensive or emergency basis.
warningRules.knownConsequence: Unlawful weapons escalation could create casualties, diplomatic crisis, and command intervention.
warningRules.availableBasisForException: A confirmed imminent hostile act could justify defensive fire.

warningRules.proposedAction: Destroy, overwrite, or broadly shut down convoy computer evidence to speed rescue.
warningRules.standardConcern: Computer state may be the only clean evidence for the false authority chain.
warningRules.knownConsequence: Evidence loss may make later authentication and accountability harder.
warningRules.availableBasisForException: Immediate life-saving need may justify a targeted shutdown if the loss is logged.

authorityNotes.summary: The XO can organize the first response and advise Whitaker; the Captain retains final legal command for major escalation.
Open detention, seizure, or jurisdictional confrontation with Compact personnel may require Whitaker unless an emergency basis is stated.

briefFacts.summary: Relief Convoy Twelve is powered and in formation, but no one answers hails.
Several escape craft are missing, and one transport has a minor plasma leak.

briefUncertainties.summary: The quarantine beacon carries a technically valid Starfleet emergency authority code that does not match current routing.
No biological signature is confirmed at range.

operationalPressures.summary: Delay may cost anyone aboard the leaking transport; closing quickly may expose the ship before the order and convoy status are verified.

decisionPoints.title: Initial Convoy Posture
decisionPoints.stakes: The XO must choose how to balance rescue speed, authentication, quarantine, security, and evidence custody before the ship commits to approach.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Do not present a menu. Officers may provide compact counsel, and Command Competence handles routine distress-response work.

decisionPoints.title: First Boarding Threshold
decisionPoints.stakes: The first irreversible contact with the convoy will determine rescue speed, evidence custody, and exposure to concealed actors.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Let the player define a threshold in natural language; warnings should surface serious departures before consequences commit.

commandDecisions.title: Own The First Response
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Inspiration, Resolve, or both only when the player makes a consequential posture choice that balances rescue, uncertainty, authority, and accepted risk without relying on a hidden correct answer.

endStates.label: Opening Response Committed
conditions: Initial response posture has been committed and pressure state is ready for the next scene.
